title: Flavorful Hearty Polish Steak Stew
description: A malty, crunchy, and comforting Polish dish featuring lean minced steak, cooked to perfection in a clay pot, perfect for a sick day, and low in carbs. This stew is a traditional Polish recipe with a twist, making it a great option for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat the clay pot over medium heat, then add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the chopped carrots and celery and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their moisture and start to brown.
Add the lean minced steak and cook until it is browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the beef broth, dark beer, tomato paste, and dried thyme, stirring to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.
Garnish with fresh parsley or thyme, if desired.
Serve with a side of cauliflower mash or a low-carb vegetable, if desired.
